note: Schema exported via DBA script. Some tables added post-go-live with no change control. FK relationships partially inferred from application code.

tables.description: Primary customer entity. WARNING: Contains both residential and business customers - no discriminator column. Business logic buried in APP_TYPE_CODE.

columns.name: CUST_ID
columns.comment: Generated by sequence CRM_CORE.CUST_SEQ

columns.name: CUST_LEGACY_ID
columns.comment: Old system ID from pre-2009 migration. NULL for post-2009 customers. Still used by billing batch jobs.

columns.name: APP_TYPE_CODE
columns.comment: RES=Residential, BUS=Business, GOV=Government, WHL=Wholesale. Drives billing logic.

columns.name: STATUS_CD
columns.comment: A=Active, S=Suspended, T=Terminated, P=Pending, X=Migrated (legacy only)

columns.name: SEGMENT_CODE
columns.comment: Marketing segment. Populated by nightly batch from MKTG schema. Often stale.

columns.name: CREDIT_CLASS
columns.comment: A1,A2,B1,B2,C1,C2,D. Used by collections and credit limit logic.

columns.name: PRIMARY_CONTACT_ID
columns.comment: FK to CONTACT_DETAILS. Nullable for migrated legacy records.

columns.name: BILLING_ACCT_ID
columns.comment: FK to BILLING_ACCOUNT in ORACLE_BILLING schema. Cross-schema dependency.

columns.name: CREATED_BY_SYS
columns.comment: CRM_UI, IVR_PORTAL, BULK_LOAD, API_GW, RETAIL_POS

columns.name: DATA_QUALITY_FLAG
columns.comment: V=Verified, U=Unverified, D=Duplicate-suspected. 38% of records are NULL here.

known_issues: ~12,000 duplicate customers identified in 2021 data quality audit - not remediated
LAST_UPD_DT not updated by all application paths (batch jobs bypass trigger)
BILLING_ACCT_ID is NULL for ~8% of active customers - breaks invoice generation

tables.description: Contact information. Multiple contacts per customer. No concept of primary vs secondary enforced at DB level.

columns.name: CUST_ID
columns.comment: FK to CUSTOMER_MASTER

columns.name: CONTACT_TYPE
columns.comment: BILLING, TECHNICAL, NOC, EXECUTIVE, PRIMARY, SECONDARY

columns.name: EMAIL_ADDR
columns.comment: Not validated at DB level. ~4% contain spaces or invalid formats.

columns.name: PREFERRED_CONTACT
columns.comment: EMAIL, SMS, PHONE, POST. NULL for 60% of records.

columns.name: CONSENT_FLAG
columns.comment: Y/N GDPR consent. Added 2018. NULL treated as Y by application - compliance risk.

known_issues: CONSENT_FLAG NULL treated as consent=given in application layer - potential GDPR violation
No unique constraint on EMAIL_ADDR + CUST_ID - same email can appear multiple times

tables.description: All service orders - activations, changes, disconnects. Core transactional table. Very high write volume.

columns.name: CUST_ID
columns.comment: FK to CUSTOMER_MASTER

columns.name: ORDER_TYPE
columns.comment: NEW_CONNECT, CHANGE, DISCONNECT, SUSPEND, RESUME, TRANSFER, PORT_IN, PORT_OUT

columns.name: ORDER_STATUS
columns.comment: PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED, HELD

columns.name: PRODUCT_CODE
columns.comment: FK to PRODUCT_CATALOG. Product catalog is in ORACLE_BILLING schema.

columns.name: COMMITTED_DT
columns.comment: SLA commitment date. NULL means no SLA assigned - happens for bulk orders.

columns.name: NC_WORK_ORDER_ID
columns.comment: Netcracker work order ID. Populated after provisioning handoff. Cross-system key.

columns.name: FALLOUT_REASON
columns.comment: Free text. No standardized codes. Used for manual fallout analysis.

columns.name: CHANNEL
columns.comment: RETAIL, TELESALES, ONLINE, DEALER, B2B_PORTAL, API, IVR, MIGRATION

columns.name: PRIORITY
columns.comment: 1=Critical, 2=High, 3=Normal, 4=Low. NULL=Normal assumed.

columns.name: PARENT_ORDER_ID
columns.comment: Self-referencing FK for complex multi-line orders.

known_issues: Table has grown 340% since 2019 - no archival strategy in place
NC_WORK_ORDER_ID sync fails ~2.3% of orders causing ghost orders in Netcracker
PARENT_ORDER_ID creates circular reference potential - no DB constraint preventing this

tables.description: Active product subscriptions per customer. The 'what does this customer have' table.

columns.name: SERVICE_ID
columns.comment: Network service identifier. Cross-reference to Netcracker resource inventory.

columns.name: STATUS
columns.comment: ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, TERMINATED, PENDING_ACTIVATION

columns.name: BILLING_START_DT
columns.comment: Often differs from ACTIVATION_DT. Billing logic depends on this delta.

columns.name: MONTHLY_CHARGE
columns.comment: Denormalized from billing. Can drift from actual billed amount.

columns.name: NC_RESOURCE_ID
columns.comment: Netcracker physical resource assignment. NULL for some legacy activations.

known_issues: MONTHLY_CHARGE denormalization causes revenue reporting discrepancies vs billing system
~15,000 records have ACTIVE status but TERMINATION_DT in the past - zombie subscriptions
NC_RESOURCE_ID NULL for 22% of broadband products - breaks network inventory reconciliation

tables.description: Audit trail for all order state changes. Massive table. No partitioning. Full table scans causing performance issues.
known_issues: No table partitioning - 94M rows causing 45-second query times on reporting jobs
Retention policy undefined - data going back to 2009
CHANGED_BY contains mix of user IDs and system names - no normalization

stored_procedures.name: SP_SUBMIT_ORDER
stored_procedures.description: Main order submission procedure. Called by CRM UI and API gateway.
stored_procedures.known_issues: Synchronous Netcracker call creates 8-12 second latency on order submission. Times out under load.

stored_procedures.name: SP_SYNC_BILLING_ACCT
stored_procedures.description: Nightly batch. Syncs BILLING_ACCT_ID from Oracle Billing back to CUSTOMER_MASTER.
calls_external: ORACLE_BILLING schema via DB link BILLINK
stored_procedures.known_issues: DB link BILLINK goes down ~3x/month causing full sync failure. No retry mechanism.

stored_procedures.name: SP_CUSTOMER_360
stored_procedures.description: Aggregation procedure for customer 360 view. Called by CRM UI on customer open.
stored_procedures.known_issues: Makes 3 synchronous external calls. P95 latency 4.2 seconds. Users complain of slow screen loads.

db_links.name: BILLINK
db_links.description: Direct DB link to Oracle Billing database. Used by 14 stored procedures.

db_links.name: NCLINK
db_links.description: Read-only link to Netcracker reporting replica.

schemas.description: Marketing schema. Managed by separate Marketing IT team. Underdocumented.

tables.description: Marketing segmentation. Updated nightly by external Teradata job via bulk load.

columns.name: CHURN_RISK_SCORE
columns.comment: 0-100. Generated by Teradata model. Refreshed weekly, not nightly.

columns.name: CLV_SCORE
columns.comment: Customer Lifetime Value estimate. Model documented in Confluence (last updated 2020).

columns.name: PROPENSITY_UPSELL
columns.comment: Upsell propensity score. Often NULL for B2B customers.

known_issues: Churn risk score and CLV score use different refresh frequencies - inconsistent combined view
No feedback loop from actual churn events back to model - model drift suspected

integration_interfaces.name: Netcracker SOAP Gateway
integration_interfaces.auth: Basic Auth (hardcoded credentials in SP_SUBMIT_ORDER - security finding)
integration_interfaces.notes: Synchronous. No circuit breaker. No retry on failure.

integration_interfaces.name: Oracle Billing DB Link
integration_interfaces.notes: Direct database link. Tight coupling. Schema changes in billing break CRM stored procedures.

integration_interfaces.name: CRM REST API (outbound)
integration_interfaces.notes: No versioning. Breaking changes deployed without consumer notification.
